title: Yosys aborts on $memrd/wr cells when saving json, reloading json, and continuing the same process
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Start Yosys and do this:
```
read_verilog CpuComplex.v
read_verilog -lib -D_ABC +/ice40/cells_sim.v
hierarchy -check -top CpuComplex   
proc
write_json CpuComplex.proc.json
exit
```

Restart Yosys and do this:
```
read_json CpuComplex.proc.json
flatten
```



Full repro environment can be found here: https://github.com/tomverbeure/yosys_error/tree/save_reload_crash.

Just clone and execute  `./run.sh`.

## Expected behavior

I expected to be able to use JSON to save and restore state and pick up where I left off.

[According](https://old.reddit.com/r/yosys/comments/fzrdje/yosys_aborts_when_saving_during_an_intermediate/fn5umzf/) to @daveshah1, the JSON netlist format shouldn't be used for that.

But at least, Yosys should bail with some error instead of just aborting.

## Actual behavior

Yosys aborts as follows:

```
...
Importing module CCPipelinedMemoryBusRam from JSON tree.
Importing module CCMasterArbiter from JSON tree.

2. Executing FLATTEN pass (flatten design).
Using template VexRiscv for cells of type VexRiscv.
<suppressed ~1 debug messages>
ERROR: Assert `memory_renames.count(memid) != 0' failed in passes/techmap/techmap.cc:425.
```

The abort is caused by the presence of `$memrd` and `$memwr` cells in the netlist. When I remove those, Yosys proceeds as expected.

## Work around

Use `write_ilang`/`read_ilang` instead of  `write_json`/`read_json`. That works fine.

The reason I wanted to use json is because it doesn't require a custom parser for most languages.
code_blocks:
...
Importing module CCPipelinedMemoryBusRam from JSON tree.
Importing module CCMasterArbiter from JSON tree.

2. Executing FLATTEN pass (flatten design).
Using template VexRiscv for cells of type VexRiscv.
<suppressed ~1 debug messages>
ERROR: Assert `memory_renames.count(memid) != 0' failed in passes/techmap/techmap.cc:425.
